summary_en: Rheon updated its mid-term plan targets to operating margin 12.4%+ and ROE 8.5%+ (from prior levels), with strategic initiatives in revenue growth, production efficiency, and shareholder returns including 40%+ payout ratio and progressive dividends.
title_en: Response to Cost of Capital and Stock Price-Conscious Management (Update)
translation_en_brief: On 20 May 2026, Rheon Automatic Machinery announced an update to its cost-of-capital and shareholder-value strategy. The company acknowledged that ROE declined 0.9 percentage points year-over-year to 9.5%, and PBR has remained below 1.0x for two consecutive periods, signalling market undervaluation. In response, management revised mid-term plan targets (FY2024–2028) to operating margin of 12.4%+ and ROE of 8.5%+, with ambitions to reach 13.0%+ and 10.0%+ respectively post-completion of a new US subsidiary factory. The improvement roadmap comprises three pillars: (1) revenue growth via smart-line and smart-factory development, new overseas market penetration, and Orange Bakery new facility launch in April 2027; (2) profit-base strengthening through production standardisation, automation, and cost-tracking systems; and (3) governance and human-capital enhancement. Dividend policy commits to a consolidated payout ratio of 40%+ and progressive dividends throughout the plan period. Enhanced IR activity and non-financial disclosure will support investor confidence and capital-cost reduction.
